title: Federal Judge Orders ICE Agent Ross's Training and Personnel Files Produced in Court
excerpt: U.S. District Judge Jeffrey M. Bryan in Minnesota ordered federal prosecutors to produce the training file, personnel records, body-camera footage, and fitness-for-duty evaluations of ICE agent Jonathan Ross — who fatally shot Renee Good — giving the government until May 1 to submit the materials for magistrate review.
content:
On April 9, 2026, U.S. District Judge Jeffrey M. Bryan issued a far-reaching discovery order in a criminal case involving a separate ICE shooting by the same agent, compelling the federal government to produce critical records bearing on the shooting of Renee Good.

  • The order commands federal prosecutors to produce: Agent Jonathan Ross's full personnel and training file; records of statements Ross made in the 60 minutes before and during Good's shooting; medical records and fitness-for-duty evaluations for Ross, including mental health records; body-worn camera footage of the incident; cell data from Ross's phone; and witness statements regarding Good's killing.
  • Prosecutors were given until May 1, 2026, to submit materials to a magistrate judge who will determine which files should be released to the defense.
  • Defense attorney Shauna Kieffer said: 'This judge is effectively doing the investigation that the United States has turned its back on,' while noting that the government might respond by dismissing the underlying case rather than comply with the order.
  • The order arose in a separate case involving another driver who had injured Ross months before Good's killing — a prior traumatic incident that legal experts noted could be highly relevant to evaluating Ross's mindset and fitness for active duty at the time he fatally shot Good.

The ruling was seen as the most significant judicial step yet toward public accountability for the January 7 killing, circumventing the Trump administration's sustained refusal to share evidence with state and local investigators.
mediaPlaceholder: Court order signed by Judge Bryan listing the ICE training records and personnel files to be produced
